paper_title: Plant and Soil Analysis for Silicon Content

methods.method_name: Plant Sample Processing and Silicon Analysis
methods.description: This method describes the process of preparing plant samples for silicon content analysis, including washing, drying, grinding, and chemical digestion of samples, followed by spectrophotometric analysis.

steps.description: Rice plants were washed with distilled water to remove soil contamination.
steps.searchable_summary: wash rice plants with distilled water to remove soil contamination

steps.description: Plant samples were dried in an oven at 75 °C until a constant dry weight was obtained.
steps.searchable_summary: dry plant samples in oven at 75°C until constant weight

steps.description: The dry plant samples were weighed, ground to a fine powder and used in further analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: weigh and grind dry plant samples to fine powder

steps.description: Panicles were separately collected and grains were separated from the panicles.
steps.searchable_summary: separate grains from panicles

steps.description: The grain weight was observed and analyzed for Si content.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze grain weight for silicon content

steps.description: The powdered grain and straw samples were dried in an oven at 70 °C for 2-3 hrs prior to analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: dry powdered samples in oven at 70°C for 2-3 hours

steps.description: The sample (0.1 g) was digested in a mixture of 7 ml of HNO3 (70%), 2 ml of H2O2 (30%) and 1 ml of HF (40%) using a microwave digestion system.
steps.searchable_summary: digest sample using microwave digestion system with acid mixture

steps.description: The digested samples were diluted to 50 ml with 4 percent boric acid.
steps.searchable_summary: dilute digested samples with boric acid

steps.description: The Si concentration in the digested solution was determined as described.
steps.searchable_summary: determine silicon concentration in digested solution

steps.description: An amount of 0.5 ml of the digested aliquot was transferred to a plastic centrifuge tube, to which was added 3.75 ml of 0.2 N HCl, 0.5 ml of 10% ammonium molybdate, 0.5 ml of 20% tartaric acid and 0.5 ml of reducing agent, and the volume was topped up to 12.5 ml with distilled water.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare solution for spectrophotometric analysis

steps.description: After one hour, the absorbance was measured at 600 nm with a UV-visible spectrophotometer.
steps.searchable_summary: measure absorbance at 600 nm using spectrophotometer

methods.description: This method outlines the analysis of soil and diatomaceous earth (DE) for physicochemical properties, including pH, electrical conductivity, nutrient content, and mineralogical composition.

steps.description: Soil pH and EC were measured in a suspension with a 1:2.5 water ratio.
steps.searchable_summary: measure soil pH and electrical conductivity in water suspension

steps.description: The soil textural class was determined by following the International Pipette method.
steps.searchable_summary: determine soil textural class using International Pipette method

steps.description: Available N was determined by the alkaline potassium permanganate method.
steps.searchable_summary: determine available nitrogen using alkaline potassium permanganate method

steps.description: Available P2O5 was estimated by Bray's method for acidic and neutral soils and by the Olsen method for alkaline soil.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate available phosphorus using Bray's or Olsen method

steps.description: Available K and exchangeable Ca and Mg were extracted using 1N ammonium acetate and K was analyzed using flame photometry.
steps.searchable_summary: extract and analyze potassium, calcium, and magnesium using ammonium acetate and flame photometry

steps.description: Exchangeable Ca and Mg were determined by the complexometric titration method.
steps.searchable_summary: determine exchangeable calcium and magnesium using complexometric titration

steps.description: Soluble silicon concentration available for plant (PAS) was determined by soil extraction using 0.01 M CaCl2, and Si was measured using UV-visible spectrophotometry.
steps.searchable_summary: determine soluble silicon concentration using CaCl2 extraction and spectrophotometry

steps.description: The field capacity of soil was determined using a pressure plate apparatus at laboratory condition.
steps.searchable_summary: determine soil field capacity using pressure plate apparatus

steps.description: The mineralogical compositions were determined by X-ray diffraction (XRD) on powdered samples.
steps.searchable_summary: determine mineralogical composition using X-ray diffraction
